examples.title: Paxos Proposer Algorithm
examples.description: Shows how a proposer initiates consensus in Paxos
examples.code:
PROCEDURE Propose(value)
BEGIN
 proposalNumber = GenerateUniqueNumber()
 
 // PREPARE PHASE
 PREPARE_MSG = CreateMessage(proposalNumber, PREPARE)
 responses = BROADCAST_TO_ALL_ACCEPTORS(PREPARE_MSG)
 
 // Check for promised proposals
 highestPromised = MAX(responses.promisedNumbers)
 IF highestPromised > proposalNumber THEN
 RETURN Reject() // Another proposer won
 ENDIF
 
 // ACCEPT PHASE 
 acceptedValue = value
 IF responses.hasAccepted THEN
 acceptedValue = responses.latestAcceptedValue
 ENDIF
 
 ACCEPT_MSG = CreateMessage(proposalNumber, acceptedValue, ACCEPT)
 acceptResponses = BROADCAST_TO_ALL_ACCEPTORS(ACCEPT_MSG)
 
 // Check for majority acceptance
 IF COUNT(acceptResponses.accepted) > n/2 THEN
 BROADCAST_TO_LEARNERS(acceptedValue)
 RETURN Success()
 ELSE
 RETURN RetryWithHigherNumber()
 ENDIF
END
examples.explanation: Proposer initiates Paxos by sending prepare with unique number. If majority promises, proposer sends accept with value. If majority accepts, consensus is reached. If rejected, retry with higher number.

examples.title: Raft Leader Election
examples.description: Shows the leader election process in Raft
examples.code:
PROCEDURE StartElection()
BEGIN
 currentTerm = currentTerm + 1
 votedFor = self.nodeId
 voteCount = 1
 state = CANDIDATE
 
 // Request votes from all other nodes
 FOR EACH peer IN allPeers DO
 IF peer.lastLogTerm > myLastLogTerm OR 
 (peer.lastLogTerm == myLastLogTerm AND 
 peer.lastLogIndex >= myLastLogIndex) THEN
 vote = RequestVote(currentTerm, myNodeId, 
 myLastLogIndex, myLastLogTerm)
 IF vote.granted THEN
 voteCount = voteCount + 1
 ENDIF
 ENDIF
 ENDFOR
 
 // If majority votes received
 IF voteCount > (totalNodes / 2) THEN
 state = LEADER
 BROADCAST_HEARTBEAT()
 FOR EACH peer IN allPeers DO
 SendAppendEntries(peer, currentTerm)
 ENDFOR
 ELSE
 // Election failed, become follower again
 state = FOLLOWER
 RestartElectionTimer()
 ENDIF
END
examples.explanation: Candidate increments term, votes for self, requests votes from peers. If peer has equal or newer log, votes are granted. If majority votes, becomes leader. Otherwise, reverts to follower.

examples.title: Two Phase Commit Protocol
examples.description: Shows atomic commit in distributed transactions
examples.code:
PROCEDURE TwoPhaseCommit(transactionId)
BEGIN
 coordinator = GetCoordinator()
 participants = GetParticipants(transactionId)
 
 // PHASE 1: PREPARE
 FOR EACH participant IN participants DO
 response = participant.CanCommit()
 IF response != YES THEN
 // Any participant votes NO
 FOR EACH p IN participants DO
 p.DoAbort()
 ENDFOR
 RETURN ABORTED
 ENDIF
 ENDFOR
 
 // All participants voted YES
 // PHASE 2: COMMIT
 FOR EACH participant IN participants DO
 participant.DoCommit()
 ENDFOR
 
 RETURN COMMITTED
END

PROCEDURE ParticipantCanCommit()
BEGIN
 IF CanLocalCommit() THEN
 WritePrepareToLog()
 RETURN YES
 ELSE
 RETURN NO
 ENDIF
END
examples.explanation: Coordinator asks all participants to prepare. If all vote YES, coordinator sends commit to all. If any votes NO, all abort. Problem: coordinator failure before commit leaves participants blocked.

examples.title: Byzantine Agreement Algorithm
examples.description: Simplified Byzantine agreement for reaching consensus with faulty nodes
examples.code:
PROCEDURE ByzantineAgree(value, f)
BEGIN
 // Assumes n >= 3f + 1 nodes
 n = totalNodes
 receivedValues = []
 
 // Round 1: Each node broadcasts its value
 FOR i = 1 TO n DO
 BROADCAST(value[i])
 ENDFOR
 
 // Collect values from all nodes
 FOR i = 1 TO n DO
 receivedValues.APPEND(value[i])
 ENDFOR
 
 // Round 2: Exchange values (to detect liars)
 exchangedValues = []
 FOR i = 1 TO n DO
 FOR j = 1 TO n DO
 IF i != j THEN
 exchangedValues[i].APPEND(
 ValueFromNode(j))
 ENDIF
 ENDFOR
 ENDFOR
 
 // Majority function: choose most common value
 decidedValue = MAJORITY(exchangedValues)
 
 // If all correct nodes follow this, 
 // they will decide the same value
 RETURN decidedValue
END

FUNCTION MAJORITY(values)
BEGIN
 counts = COUNT_OCCURRENCES(values)
 RETURN value with highest count
END
examples.explanation: Simplified Byzantine agreement requires 3f+1 nodes. With f traitors, 2f correct nodes can identify the majority value despite f false values. Each round exchanges values to detect inconsistencies.
